title: Guardian (LLM-as-judge) output
description: Structured output of a guardian_step. Tool-use schema enforced at LLM call site (Anthropic tool use / OpenAI structured outputs). Engine performs additional semantic validation against context (round echo, evidence coverage, target whitelist).

decision.description: The single binding choice. Engine maps this to a transition via the guardian_step's outputs map.

summary.description: 1-line summary used as commit subject and progress event message.

reasoning.description: Free-form rationale. Audited but never consumed by transitions.

round.description: Echo of the round counter passed in. Engine asserts equality with its own context.round.

blocking_findings.description: Required (and non-empty) iff decision == repair_needed. Consumed verbatim by repair node.

non_blocking_findings.description: Optional. Logged for the record but does not gate transitions.

evidence_consumed.description: Node ids whose output the guardian read. Engine asserts this matches the spawned reviewer set; missing reviewer ids → GuardianViolation.

next_target_override.description: Optional override of the default decision→target mapping. Engine validates against the guardian_step's allowed_targets (= union of all output target node ids).

evidence_ref.description: Pointer to the supporting evidence: 'node_id', 'path:line', or '<file>#<section>'. Free string by design (no parsing burden on the LLM).

raised_by.description: Reviewer node id that originally surfaced this finding. Aggregator preserves attribution.

recommended_action.description: How the finding should be handled. fix_now is the default for blocking findings; the others are valid when the guardian explicitly supplies them.
